title: PodRmReport.RmReport.Err: cannot unmarshal from json string
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

`json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field PodRmReport.RmReport.Err of type error`

Calling two instances of binding.pods.Remove simultaneously will trigger this bug.

If the RmReport Err field is set, the json unmarshal fails:

The root cause is that the RmReport Err field is a Error type which cannot be unmarshaled from a json string.

https://github.com/paralin/podman/blob/aperture/pkg/bindings/pods/pods.go#L194

comments.body: Interested in opening a PR to fix?

It's fixable by changing the field from Error to String or by using an intermediate type to unmarshal, or by overriding UnmarshalJSON and MarshalJSON.

Which approach makes most sense here?

Since this probably also is the case with other types in that file - StartReport etc - it's most likely best to just change all of those `error` to `string` in any type that might be unmarshaled from json.

Alternatively, could add a custom 'error' type which has UnmarshalJSON.

We should not change the type, pkg/bindings is stable. I agree that this also affects other endpoints.
I have no real preference over using a custom UnmarshalJSON or a intermediate type.

We have 
```
jsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc("error", MarshalErrorJSON, MarshalErrorJSONIsEmpty)
jsoniter.RegisterTypeEncoderFunc("[]error", MarshalErrorSliceJSON, MarshalErrorSliceJSONIsEmpty
```
already. I think `jsoniter.RegisterTypeDecoderFunc()` is the best way to fix this issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan Team has to make decision on path to take regarding using error types in API responses. I provided a document on the associated Jira card.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This issue has fallen throught the cracks because of the stale-issue flag.  I believe this is still an issue.
